project_overview.project_name: Smallholder Commercialization and Agribusiness Development Project
project_overview.project_development_objective: To promote smallholder commercialization by fostering productive business linkages between smallholder farmers and selected agribusiness firms and other commodity off-takers in Sierra Leone
project_overview.total_financing: US$55 million (IDA: US$40 million; DFID: US$15 million)
project_overview.beneficiaries: 50,000 smallholder farmers (40% women and youth), agribusinesses, SMEs, government officials, and institutions providing services for smallholder commercialization

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Sierra Leone is on the 2013/14 harmonized list of Fragile and Conflict Affected Situations (FCS) countries; project designed to support post-Ebola recovery

key_components.component_name: Support for agribusiness-farmer linkages and SMEs along selected agricultural value chains
key_components.description: Strengthens linkages between agribusiness firms and farmers through out-grower models and farmer aggregation models. Includes Sierra Leone Agribusiness Development Fund (SLADF) for value chain financing, support to Apex Bank for farmer credit, and matching grants for SMEs. Targets rice, cocoa, oil palm, and poultry value chains.

Rehabilitation of 500-600 km of feeder roads linking production areas to markets using performance-based contracts. Support for aggregation structures and ICT technologies for market coordination. Includes capacity building for road maintenance.
key_components.financing: US$26.00 million (IDA: US$11 million; DFID: US$15 million)

key_components.component_name: Capacity building support for state and non-state institutions and producer organizations
key_components.description: Technical assistance and capacity building for MAFFS, SLARI, Ministry of Trade and Industry, SLIEPA, SLeCAD, NaFFSL, SLRA, and community banks. Includes long-term training (MSc/PhD), short-term training, and value-chain development services.
Project Coordination Unit operations, staff salaries, equipment, monitoring and evaluation framework, baseline and impact evaluation studies, and management information system.

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Agriculture, Forestry and Food Security (MAFFS) through Project Coordination Unit (PCU); Sierra Leone Roads Authority (SLRA) for feeder roads; Fund Manager for SLADF; Apex Bank for agricultural credit
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: National Project Steering Committee (NPSC) chaired by Minister of Agriculture; Advisory Committee for SLADF; partnership with IFC, FAO, IFAD, and DFID for technical support; bi-annual joint implementation support missions
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank procurement guidelines; National Competitive Bidding (NCB) for goods and works; International Competitive Bidding (ICB) for large contracts; Shopping for smaller items; Consultants selected through QCBS, QBS, FBS, LCS, or CQS methods
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: PCU responsible for M&E; quarterly interim financial reports; annual audited financial statements; baseline survey, mid-term evaluation, and end-of-project evaluation; Management Information System (MIS) for data collection and analysis

identified_risks.risk_description: Political interference in project implementation and fund management; weak legislative scrutiny; declining government revenues affecting fiscal transfers
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Independent project financial management unit with Bank-paid staff; annual external audits; regular FM reviews by Bank team; PFM reform efforts by Government

identified_risks.risk_description: Economic slowdown from Ebola epidemic; declining commodity prices (iron ore); reduced foreign direct investment; currency fluctuations
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Project designed to support post-Ebola recovery; diversified commodity value chains; focus on domestic investment; flexible implementation timeline

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak agricultural sector policies; inadequate public investment in research and extension; insufficient feeder roads maintenance budget; limited agribusiness policy framework
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Support for policy development (agribusiness strategy, feeder roads policy review); capacity building for policy institutions; establishment of Agribusiness Forum for policy dialogue

identified_risks.risk_description: Complexity of implementing multiple components with different implementing agencies; challenges in identifying and selecting appropriate feeder roads; risks in piloting Output and Performance-based Road Contracts (OPRC); low productivity affecting farmer defaults on contracts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Detailed Project Implementation Manual; early road identification and selection; Asset Management Strategy study; technology support and capacity building; supply contracts with win-win arrangements; sensitization of farmers and agribusinesses

identified_risks.risk_category: institutional capacity for implementation and sustainability
identified_risks.risk_description: Weak institutional capacity at PCU and implementing agencies; limited experience with Bank procedures at some agencies; capacity constraints from Ebola epidemic; challenges in sustaining activities beyond project support
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Use of existing PCU with proven experience; capacity building for all implementing agencies; recruitment of qualified staff; technical assistance from development partners; focus on building government capacity for sustainability

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak financial management systems; inadequate internal controls; delays in financial reporting; limited audit capacity; risks of fraud and corruption; weak procurement capacity at some agencies
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: FM assessment and risk mitigation plan; enhanced accountability framework; internal and external audits; spot checks; social accountability tools; procurement capacity building; simplified procedures for FCS context

identified_risks.risk_description: Potential environmental impacts from increased pesticide/fertilizer use; land acquisition issues with agribusinesses; risk of elite capture and exclusion of vulnerable groups; child labor risks; displacement risks from road construction; impacts on natural habitats and forests
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Environmental and Social Management Framework (ESMF); Integrated Pest Management Plan; Resettlement Policy Framework; land acquisition audit for agribusinesses; grievance redress mechanism; gender and youth targeting; environmental screening of subprojects; monitoring by EPA

identified_risks.risk_description: Coordination challenges among multiple implementing agencies; weak farmer organization capacity; limited private sector engagement; potential conflicts between agribusinesses and smallholder farmers
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Clear roles and responsibilities in PIM; capacity building for farmer organizations; engagement of agribusinesses in project design; value-chain development services; Agribusiness Forum for stakeholder coordination

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project-level GRM with multiple uptake locations and channels; service standards for resolution; clear processing guidelines; based on principles of fairness, objectivity, simplicity, responsiveness, speed, and social inclusion. Also access to World Bank's corporate GRS and Inspection Panel
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Target of 40% women and youth beneficiaries; gender disaggregation in key performance indicators; Gender Specialist in PCU; specific mechanisms to target women and youth in fund and matching grant selection; at least 30% of agricultural loan funds for women-only groups; at least 50% of matching grant funds for women and youth

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed to support post-Ebola recovery and contribute to peace-building through inclusive economic growth. Addresses livelihood constraints that could exacerbate conflict. Includes grievance redress mechanism to address community concerns. Focuses on building productive partnerships between farmers and agribusinesses to reduce tensions. Avoids areas with existing land conflicts.
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Sierra Leone recovering from 10-year civil war (ended 2002) and Ebola epidemic (2014-2015). Weak institutional capacity, limited government revenue, high poverty levels, youth unemployment, and limited economic opportunities. Agriculture supports 80% of rural population but operates at subsistence level. Project aims to transform agriculture into engine for inclusive growth and poverty reduction.
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Project implemented in post-conflict context with ongoing security challenges. Feeder roads rehabilitation may improve security by enhancing connectivity. Labor-based road maintenance scheduled during low agricultural demand (December-March) to minimize labor migration risks. Project includes awareness training for construction workers on available support services.
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: no - Project explicitly designed to avoid involuntary resettlement. Feeder roads rehabilitation on existing road footprints only. Replanting activities on existing farms and plantations only. Agribusinesses with existing or potential land acquisition conflicts are ineligible. However, Resettlement Policy Framework prepared as precautionary measure to address any unforeseen circumstances.
